title: Lean: expTree_evalReal + depth_k_unbounded reformulation

new_lemmas.name: expTree_evalReal
new_lemmas.statement: ∀ x : ℝ, expTree.evalReal x = Real.exp x

new_lemmas.name: EMLTree.HasVar
new_lemmas.statement: Predicate: tree contains at least one .var leaf

new_lemmas.name: no_var_evalReal_const
new_lemmas.statement: ¬HasVar t → ∃ c, ∀ x, t.evalReal x = c
new_lemmas.sorry_note: ceml inductive case: x-independence of eval for no-var trees

new_lemmas.name: depth_k_unbounded_along_real
new_lemmas.statement: HasVar t → 1 ≤ depth → Tendsto evalReal atTop atTop ∨ atBot
new_lemmas.sorry_note: General inductive step. Base case (expTree) proved. Im-part bound needed for ceml case.

sorry_count_change.note: Same count but CORRECTLY STATED now; original lemma was false

key_insight: depth_k_unbounded_along_real required HasVar predicate. Without it, ceml(const 0, const(-1)) is a depth-1 counterexample with constant evalReal=1. This is mathematically important: the barrier against sin is really about trees that FUNCTIONALLY depend on x, not just have high depth.
